skill.name: reference-builder
skill.description: Creates complete technical reference documentation for APIs, configuration options, schemas, and developer-facing specifications.
skill.summary: Builds structured, searchable reference documentation for APIs, settings, schemas, and technical systems.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged backticks, weak cryptography keywords, and reconnaissance wording in SKILL.md. Review found these are Markdown formatting and documentation terminology, with no executable code, shell command instructions, network access, credential access, or prompt injection evidence.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings at SKILL.md lines 26, 64, 77, and 83 are Markdown inline code, a fenced documentation template, and parameter examples. They do not instruct the agent to execute shell commands or run external programs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are visible Markdown documentation examples. No shell command, interpreter invocation, or user-controlled execution path appears in the file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keywords in Documentation Text
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings point to ordinary documentation words such as description, default, deprecated, and exhaustive. No cryptographic algorithm use or recommendation appears in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is prose about reference documentation fields and sections. I did not find evidence of DES, MD5, SHA1, or another weak algorithm being used or promoted.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Wording
low_findings.description: The low-severity reconnaissance finding maps to configuration documentation text about environment-specific settings. It does not request host enumeration, system probing, or collection of machine details.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are part of a configuration guide checklist. There is no command, data collection step, or instruction to inspect the runtime environment.

content.value_statement: Teams often lose time when APIs, schemas, and settings are documented inconsistently. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code create structured reference material that is easier to search and maintain.
actual_capabilities: Defines structures for API references with methods, parameters, return values, errors, and examples.
Guides configuration documentation with defaults, valid ranges, dependencies, and migration notes.
Organizes schema references around fields, validation rules, relationships, indexes, and versioning.
Adds navigation aids such as tables of contents, indexes, cross-references, and search terms.
Promotes complete reference sections for quick starts, troubleshooting, warnings, and migration guides.
Supports Markdown-first output with optional metadata for schemas, OpenAPI, and type definitions.
limitations: It provides documentation guidance and structure, not automatic code parsing by itself.
It depends on accurate source material from the user or repository.
The referenced implementation playbook is not included in the scanned file tree.
It does not verify external API behavior unless the agent is given reliable evidence.

use_cases.title: Document a Public API
use_cases.description: Create a complete API reference with endpoints, parameters, responses, errors, authentication, and examples.

use_cases.title: Standardize Configuration Guides
use_cases.description: Turn scattered setting notes into a searchable reference with defaults, valid ranges, dependencies, and migration guidance.

use_cases.title: Publish Schema References
use_cases.description: Describe fields, constraints, relationships, indexes, and version changes for data models or message formats.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Reference
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for a small API or module.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the reference-builder skill to create a Markdown reference for this API. Include each method, parameter, return value, error case, and one simple example.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Configuration Guide
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the reference-builder skill to document these configuration options. Include defaults, valid values, environment differences, dependencies, warnings, and migration notes.

prompt_templates.title: Organize a Schema Reference
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for data contracts or database schemas.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the reference-builder skill to create a schema reference. Cover field types, constraints, relationships, indexes, validation rules, examples, and version history.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Reference Completeness
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for improving existing documentation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the reference-builder skill to review this reference documentation for missing entries, unclear parameters, weak examples, missing edge cases, and searchability gaps.

output_examples.input: A list of API methods and parameter notes for a payment service.
output_examples.output: A structured API reference with method sections, parameter tables, response descriptions, error mappings, authentication notes, examples, and cross-links.

output_examples.input: A scattered set of deployment settings from a README and issue tracker.
output_examples.output: A configuration reference with defaults, required flags, valid ranges, environment-specific notes, dependencies, warnings, and migration guidance.

output_examples.input: A data model with fields, relationships, indexes, and validation rules.
output_examples.output: A schema reference that explains each field, constraint, relationship, index, version change, and practical usage example.

best_practices: Provide source material for every public method, option, field, error, and version change.
Ask for tables, examples, warnings, and cross-references when the reference will serve developers.
Review generated documentation against the implementation before publishing it as a source of truth.
anti_patterns: Do not ask it to invent undocumented behavior or hidden limits.
Do not use it for narrative tutorials when a task needs a concise reference only.
Do not publish generated references without checking names, defaults, examples, and version details.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates structured technical references for APIs, configuration options, schemas, and complete specifications.

faq.question: Does it generate runnable code?
faq.answer: It can include examples when source details are provided, but its main purpose is documentation structure and completeness.

faq.question: Can it document configuration settings?
faq.answer: Yes. It guides documentation for defaults, valid ranges, dependencies, environment settings, and migration paths.

faq.question: Can it produce OpenAPI or schema metadata?
faq.answer: The skill supports metadata inclusion as a documentation target, but users must provide accurate source details.

faq.question: Is it safe for marketplace publication?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed file contains documentation instructions only and no confirmed dangerous execution, network, or prompt injection behavior.

faq.question: What should users provide for best results?
faq.answer: Users should provide implementation details, public interfaces, examples, error cases, version information, and known constraints.
